test.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... planning structural validation for gemini-extension.json, commands/mission-plan.toml, skills/mission-planner/SKILL.md, skills/define-worker-skills/SKILL.md, GEMINI.md ... PY

typecheck.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... tomllib/frontmatter validation for commands/*.toml and skills/*/SKILL.md ... PY

lint.command: gemini extensions uninstall gemini-mission-control && gemini extensions link /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension

blockingIssues.description: skills/define-worker-skills/SKILL.md instructs planners to create worker definitions under .mission/skills/<worker-name>/SKILL.md instead of agents/*.md, which conflicts with this mission's architecture and validation contract for mission-worker/code-quality-worker agent definitions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document where project-local helper skills live and how workers should use them when the runtime Skill tool cannot activate a repo-local .factory/skills entry.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three planning reviews noted worker attempts to invoke extension-builder via the Skill tool failed even though .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md exists in the repository.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Standardize guidance on where mission-specific worker definitions belong so planner/worker skills do not disagree between agents/*.md and .mission/skills/*/SKILL.md.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The define-worker-skills review found GEMINI.md and architecture guidance pointing to agents/*.md while skills/define-worker-skills/SKILL.md directed planners to .mission/skills/<worker-name>/SKILL.md.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated extension-builder skill activation failures across planning workers
rejectedObservations.reason: Deduplicated into one systemic guidance update instead of recording the same runtime-discovery issue three times.
